ideas.title: Correlating Ultrafast Carrier Dynamics with 3D Atomic-Scale Chemistry in Nanoprecipitates
ideas.description: This research direction combines ultrafast spectroscopy and atom probe tomography (APT) on the same set of semiconductor samples containing Ge-rich nanoprecipitates. Ultrafast optical or X-ray probes will first map the carrier recombination dynamics and identify specific quenching sites with high temporal resolution. Subsequently, targeted lift-out and APT analysis of the exact regions probed spectroscopically will provide a 3D atomic map of the chemical identity, distribution, and local strain fields of the precipitates. This direct correlation allows us to definitively assign specific non-radiative pathways—such as trap-assisted Auger processes or defect-mediated recombination—to specific atomic-scale chemical features like precipitate interfaces, composition gradients, or impurity clusters, moving beyond ensemble averaging.

ideas.title: Suppress local distortions via templated synthesis inspired by protein folding chaperones
ideas.description: This idea adapts a solution from structural biology, where molecular chaperones solve the problem of protein misfolding by providing a templated environment. The analogous problem in perovskite synthesis is the misfolding of the crystal structure into a non-luminescent phase due to local distortions (octahedral tilting, lone pair expression) in high-Br compositions. The adapted research idea is to develop synthetic strategies using rigid molecular or nanoporous templates as 'synthetic chaperones.' These templates would pre-organize precursor interactions and guide crystallization, sterically and electronically suppressing the local distortions that quench photoluminescence, thereby recovering efficient emission in high-Br compositions.

ideas.title: Mapping the Strain-Phonon Phase Diagram of Quantum Materials
ideas.description: This research direction combines thin-film epitaxial strain engineering with high-resolution inelastic neutron scattering to systematically probe how tunable, uniform chemical pressure induces phonon mode softening in quantum materials. By fabricating a series of epitaxially strained thin films on substrates with varying lattice constants, one can apply a continuous, clean strain field as a control parameter. Using inelastic neutron scattering, the complete phonon dispersion can be measured directly for each strain state, revealing how specific soft modes evolve and potentially condense to drive phase transitions. This approach enables the construction of a definitive strain-phonon phase diagram, uncovering new metastable states and providing a direct mechanistic link between engineered strain, lattice dynamics, and emergent quantum phenomena.

ideas.title: Atom Probe Tomography of Ge-Rich Nanoprecipitates to Directly Probe Non-Monotonic Bandgap Evolution
ideas.description: This research direction combines atom probe tomography (APT) for 3D nanoscale chemical mapping with targeted electronic structure analysis to investigate if bandgap evolution in semiconductor alloys is truly smooth. By applying APT to precisely map the composition and interface structure of Ge-rich nanoprecipitates in a SiGe matrix, we can identify local regions undergoing compositional or strain-induced phase transitions. Subsequent local electronic property measurements (e.g., via scanning tunneling spectroscopy on the same or correlated regions) will test for discontinuities in the bandgap directly linked to the atomic-scale structural features revealed by APT. This approach uniquely bridges the gap between nanoscale chemical tomography and electronic property measurement, enabling a direct, causal test of the smooth bandgap assumption at the relevant length scale of individual precipitates.

ideas.title: Air-Stable SnTe Nanocrystal Thin Films via Integrated Passivation and Controlled-Environment Fabrication
ideas.description: This project investigates chemical pressure effects in SnTe nanocrystals by first solving the fatal material instability problem. It develops a controlled material processing pipeline where SnTe nanocrystals are synthesized and immediately functionalized with a suite of novel, air-stabilizing ligand and shell coatings. These stabilized nanocrystals are then processed into thin films entirely within an inert atmosphere glovebox system equipped with integrated spin-coating and annealing capabilities. The resulting encapsulated, air-stable devices allow for reliable measurement of the intrinsic electronic and thermoelectric properties modulated by quantum confinement and ligand-induced chemical pressure, providing a robust platform for practical device exploration.

ideas.title: Relax single-phase material assumption

ideas.title: Quantifying Dynamic Lone Pair Expression via Anisotropic 119Sn NMR Tensor Mapping
ideas.description: This research program moves beyond qualitative descriptions by defining the stereochemical activity of the Sn(II) lone pair through the directly measurable anisotropy of the 119Sn NMR chemical shift tensor. A computational pipeline first predicts how specific ligand-field-induced geometric distortions modulate the lone pair's spatial distribution and, consequently, the NMR tensor's principal components. A targeted series of sterically and electronically tuned Sn(II) complexes are then synthesized to experimentally span the predicted distortion space. Direct correlation between the computed and experimentally measured 119Sn shift tensors provides the first rigorous, spectroscopic quantification of lone pair expression as a tunable electronic property.

ideas.title: Predictive Stability Framework for Ge-rich Nanoprecipitates Under Coupled Electro-Thermal Operating Conditions
ideas.description: This research establishes a mechanism-based, predictive framework for the stability of Ge-rich nanoprecipitates by investigating their evolution under simultaneous thermal and electrical stress. Using in-situ TEM for electro-thermal biasing and atom probe tomography, the study quantifies precipitate coarsening, compositional drift, and interface degradation in real-time. These experimental results are used to parameterize a coupled phase-field and electronic property model that predicts long-term microstructural evolution and its impact on optoelectronic performance. The outcome is a validated stability map that defines safe operating conditions and predicts device lifetime, moving beyond descriptive study to enable design-for-reliability.

ideas.title: High-Dimensional Compositional Tuning via Chemical Pressure Landscapes
ideas.description: This idea adapts high-dimensional optimization strategies from machine learning to explore chemical pressure in complex SnTe-based ternary or quaternary systems. The analogous problem of navigating a vast, multi-parameter space to find an optimum—solved in ML via surrogate modeling and active learning—is applied here. We propose to treat the compositional space of these alloys as a high-dimensional 'chemical pressure landscape.' By employing a sequential design-of-experiments approach, starting with a sparse but strategically chosen set of compositions, we will build a predictive model linking composition to key metrics (e.g., band gap, lattice thermal conductivity). This model will then iteratively guide the synthesis of new samples, efficiently mapping the landscape to discover compositions where chemical pressure optimally tunes the material's functional properties, thereby scaling the investigation of complexity in a targeted, resource-effective manner.

ideas.title: Predicting Nanoprecipitate Stability via Sequence-to-Property ML Models
ideas.description: This idea adapts the machine learning solution from computational biology, where sequence-to-property models predict protein stability, to the problem of predicting nanoprecipitate formation. In the analogous field, models are trained on vast databases of amino acid sequences and their measured properties. Here, we adapt this by treating the compositional 'sequence' of a potential nanoprecipitate (derived from phase diagram regions and known crystal structures) as the model input. We propose training a deep learning model, such as a graph neural network adept at handling non-sequential structural data, on these material 'sequences' paired with stability labels. This adapted approach aims to learn the complex, high-dimensional mapping between multi-element compositional space and precipitate stability, enabling the prediction of novel, high-performance nanoprecipitates for alloy design.

ideas.title: Adapting Hydrostatic Pressure Cells for Epitaxial Strain Control
ideas.description: This idea adapts a solution from high-pressure physics—the use of hydrostatic pressure cells to apply uniform, tunable chemical pressure—to the context of thin-film strain engineering. In the analogous field, diamond anvil cells with a pressure-transmitting medium apply isotropic pressure to study material phases. The adapted solution is to design an epitaxial heterostructure where the target material is grown within a soft, compliant buffer layer that acts as a 'strain-transmitting medium.' By engineering the lattice constant and elastic properties of this buffer layer, one can apply a continuous, uniform biaxial strain (analogous to hydrostatic pressure in two dimensions) to the film. This method allows for precise, tunable chemical pressure without the fixed, discrete strain states typically imposed by rigid substrates, enabling new pathways to control electronic, magnetic, and superconducting properties.

ideas.title: Apply High-Throughput DFT Screening with a Multi-Fidelity Surrogate Model for Dopant Discovery
ideas.description: This idea adapts the multi-fidelity screening pipeline common in drug discovery to the problem of identifying optimal small-radius dopant combinations via computational screening. In the analogous field, fast, low-fidelity models act as filters to manage the vast chemical space before applying expensive high-fidelity simulations. The adapted solution is to implement a two-stage computational workflow: first, a rapid, low-fidelity surrogate model (such as a coarse-grained machine learning potential, a semi-empirical method, or a rule-based heuristic considering ionic radii and electronegativity) performs an initial high-throughput sweep of a large dopant candidate space. This stage eliminates clearly non-viable options. Second, the drastically reduced subset of promising candidates is then evaluated using accurate, high-throughput Density Functional Theory (DFT) calculations. This hybrid approach maximizes the efficiency of the screening process, allowing researchers to explore a significantly broader design space of dopants without a proportional increase in computational cost, thereby accelerating the discovery of optimal materials for applications like semiconductors or catalysts.

ideas.title: ML-Guided Design of Phonon-Engineered Nanostructures via Resonant Ultrasound Spectroscopy
ideas.description: This research fuses machine learning (ML) prediction of stable nanoprecipitate compositions with resonant ultrasound spectroscopy (RUS) quantification of phonon mode softening. The ML models, trained on thermodynamic and kinetic data, propose candidate nanostructures predicted to induce specific lattice strain fields. These candidates are then synthesized, and their actual phonon spectra are measured with high precision using RUS. The RUS data, providing direct experimental feedback on defect-phonon interactions, is used to iteratively retrain and improve the ML models, creating a physics-informed feedback loop. This synergy enables the targeted design of alloy nanostructures not just for thermodynamic stability, but for precise control over thermal conductivity, mechanical damping, and other phonon-mediated properties.

ideas.title: Multi-Scale Structural Hierarchy Mapping for Predictive Optoelectronic Property Distributions
ideas.description: This approach moves beyond a simple local-versus-average dichotomy to define and quantify the hierarchical structural landscape of a material. By performing correlated, multi-modal microscopy across atomic, nano-, and mesoscales on the same sample coordinates, it builds a comprehensive structural map. This multi-scale dataset is then integrated with spatially resolved optoelectronic property maps through causal inference and machine learning models. The outcome is a predictive framework that identifies which specific structural features, and their cross-scale couplings, are the primary determinants of key property metrics like photoluminescence quantum yield and charge carrier mobility.

ideas.title: Applying Metallurgical Phase Diagram Analysis to Halide Mixing in Perovskites
ideas.description: This idea challenges the default assumption of ideal solid solutions in halide-alloyed perovskites by drawing an analogy from metallurgy. In alloy science, the simplistic ideal solution model was superseded by empirical phase diagram construction, revealing complex non-ideal behavior like compound formation and phase separation. Adapted to perovskites, the research would involve systematic experimental mapping of mixed-halide phase diagrams using in-situ structural and thermal analysis to identify and quantify regions of non-ideality, such as halide segregation or ordered phases, thereby providing a more accurate thermodynamic framework for understanding and engineering these materials.

ideas.title: Scalable Processing of Metastable Ge-Rich Nanoprecipitates for Stable Thermoelectric Performance
ideas.description: This research direction integrates the study of nanoprecipitate stability with scalable powder metallurgy to develop a processing-structure-stability framework. It will investigate how key industrial powder processing steps—such as milling, consolidation, and sintering—affect the formation kinetics and thermal degradation of metastable Ge-rich nanoprecipitates in SnTe. The goal is to identify and optimize scalable processing windows that not only produce material at tonnage scales but also inherently yield a microstructure with enhanced long-term stability under operating temperatures, ensuring durable thermoelectric performance in commercial devices.

ideas.title: Decoupling Chemical Bonding from Pure Volume Effects on Mechanical Properties via Matched Lattice Constant Studies
ideas.description: This research systematically isolates the influence of lattice volume from chemical bonding on mechanical strength and toughness. It achieves this by performing nanoindentation and microfracture tests on samples under tunable hydrostatic pressure in a diamond anvil cell and on a series of chemically substituted compounds engineered to match those same lattice parameters. The direct comparison between properties under physical pressure and chemical pressure at identical unit cell volumes quantifies the distinct role of altered electronic structure. This approach reveals whether mechanical enhancement is primarily a consequence of reduced atomic spacing or a secondary effect of modified bond character due to chemical substitution.

ideas.title: AI-Driven Process Control for Scalable Synthesis of Precipitate-Engineered Thermoelectric Powders
ideas.description: This research integrates machine learning and scalable powder processing to establish an intelligent manufacturing platform for doped SnTe thermoelectric compounds. ML models, trained on thermodynamic and kinetic data, will predict the processing windows needed to form specific, performance-enhancing nanoprecipitates during industrial-scale powder synthesis and consolidation. The key innovation is using these predictions to actively control and optimize the parameters of tonnage-capable powder metallurgy routes, such as high-energy ball milling and spark plasma sintering, ensuring the targeted nanostructures are consistently reproduced. This enables the first scalable, quality-assured production of next-generation thermoelectric materials with designer nanoprecipitate microstructures.

ideas.title: Adaptive Ultrafast Spectroscopy with Local Structure Feedback
ideas.description: This idea adapts the computational sorting and classification techniques from cryo-EM single-particle analysis to the domain of ultrafast spectroscopy. In cryo-EM, heterogeneous conformational states are resolved from a massive dataset of 2D projections. Similarly, we propose to acquire vast datasets of single-shot ultrafast spectroscopic measurements (e.g., using high-repetition-rate lasers and array detectors) on materials exhibiting photoluminescence quenching. A machine learning-based analysis pipeline would then classify these snapshots into distinct groups representing different instantaneous local structural environments (distortions). By analyzing the non-radiative recombination kinetics (e.g., decay components) associated with each classified structural group, we can directly establish causal links between specific, transient local atomic configurations and the efficiency of competing non-radiative pathways, moving beyond ensemble-averaged descriptions of dynamics.

Using inelastic neutron scattering, the experiment will directly map how this localized perturbation selectively softens particular phonon modes, revealing the detailed mechanism and spatial propagation of phonon mode softening in materials, analogous to tracing allosteric effects in proteins.

ideas.title: Transfer Atom Probe Tomography via Cryo-FIB Lift-Out for Nanoprecipitate Analysis
ideas.description: This idea adapts the 'cryo-FIB lift-out' technique from structural biology to materials science for superior nanoprecipitate analysis. In cryo-electron microscopy, cryogenic FIB milling is used to extract pristine, site-specific samples from frozen-hydrated cells to preserve their native state. By transferring this method, we propose preparing atom probe tomography specimens under cryogenic conditions to analyze Ge-rich nanoprecipitates. This adapted approach prevents artifact-inducing thermal diffusion during standard room-temperature FIB preparation, thereby enabling a more faithful 3D chemical mapping of the nanoprecipitates' true size, distribution, and composition as they exist in the bulk material.

ideas.title: Strain-Transfer via Freestanding Nanomembrane Stamps for Universal Chemical Pressure
ideas.description: This approach adapts semiconductor membrane release and transfer techniques to create a universal strain-engineering platform. A thin, single-crystal oxide or semiconductor layer is grown epitaxially on a sacrificial substrate, subjected to epitaxial strain, and then released as a freestanding nanomembrane. This pre-strained 'stamp' is subsequently transferred and bonded onto the surface of a target material—including polycrystalline, air-sensitive, or complex bulk samples—imparting a controlled biaxial strain via direct elastic interaction. This method decouples the strain application from materials compatibility constraints, enabling tunable chemical pressure across a vastly broader range of material systems than traditional epitaxial strain alone.

ideas.title: Mapping Chemical Pressure to Mechanical Properties via a Bio-Inspired Strain-Stiffening Framework
ideas.description: This research adapts a solution from biophysics, where the mechanical phase diagram of cytoskeletal networks is mapped by varying cross-linker concentration and measuring the resulting mechanical response. The analogous problem here is understanding how lattice shrinkage from chemical pressure affects mechanical properties. The adapted idea is to treat chemical pressure-inducing dopants like cross-linkers, creating a material series with controlled lattice contraction. By employing high-throughput nanoindentation and in-situ microcantilever fracture tests, the study will generate a functional map that quantitatively correlates the degree of chemical pressure (quantified by lattice parameter) with the evolution of elastic modulus, yield strength, and fracture toughness, revealing any thresholds or non-linear stiffening behaviors analogous to biological networks.

ideas.title: Closed-loop adaptive manufacturing for reproducible high-zT SnTe thermoelectrics
ideas.description: This project develops a scalable, adaptive manufacturing platform for doped SnTe thermoelectric materials that guarantees reproducible high performance. It combines scalable powder metallurgy with integrated in-line sensors to monitor critical powder characteristics like composition and size in real-time. This sensor data feeds a process model that dynamically adjusts consolidation parameters (sintering, hot-pressing) to correct for batch variations and achieve the precise microstructure needed for high zT. The outcome is a robust industrial route that directly links process control to final thermoelectric property validation, ensuring tonnage production of material that meets strict performance specifications.

ideas.title: Closed-loop multi-fidelity screening with robotic validation for robust dopant discovery
ideas.description: This system addresses the accuracy gap in pure DFT screening by implementing an iterative, closed-loop pipeline. It employs a tiered computational strategy, from high-throughput semi-empirical methods to high-accuracy corrections, to efficiently prioritize candidate dopants. An integrated robotic lab autonomously synthesizes and characterizes the most promising predicted materials. The experimental outcomes are then used to retrain and correct the computational models, creating a self-improving discovery platform that directly grounds predictions in empirical reality.

ideas.title: FIB-Transferred Site-Specific Nanoprecipitate Analysis via Atom Probe Tomography
ideas.description: This approach overcomes the fatal flaw of traditional APT specimen preparation by implementing a robust transfer technique. A region containing a Ge-rich nanoprecipitate, identified via TEM or SEM, is extracted using a FIB microsampling needle and transferred to a pre-fabricated silicon microtip array. The extracted lamella is welded onto a sharpened tip post, and annular milling is used to finalize a needle-shaped APT specimen centered on the precipitate. This method guarantees site-specific analysis, enables the study of precipitates in fragile matrices, and significantly improves the yield of successful APT runs for statistically meaningful nanoprecipitate characterization.

ideas.title: Adapting Pharmaceutical Continuous Manufacturing for Scalable Powder Synthesis
ideas.description: To achieve scalable, tonnage production of doped SnTe thermoelectric compounds, this idea adapts the continuous manufacturing (CM) framework successfully implemented in pharmaceutical production. The analogous pharmaceutical solution replaced discrete, variable batch processes with integrated, modular flow lines featuring real-time Process Analytical Technology (PAT) for quality control at scale. The adapted research idea develops a modular, continuous powder processing route. This system would integrate unit operations for continuous synthesis (e.g., spray pyrolysis), precise in-stream doping, and direct powder conditioning/consolidation into a single flow line. By incorporating in-line sensors for real-time monitoring of composition, particle size, and morphology, this approach aims to ensure consistent, high-quality powder production at industrial throughputs, fundamentally shifting the scaling paradigm from sequential batch steps to a controlled, efficient continuous process.
